channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇫🇮 Finland raises concerns with Ukraine over drones entering Finnish airspace
item.description: Finnish PM Petteri Orpo told Zelensky that drone incursions near the Finnish-Russian border are unacceptable. The incident coincided with an alleged Ukrainian drone strike on Leningrad Oblast.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil prices climb as Iran threatens US forces entering Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Brent crude rose 1.5% to $109.80 after Iran warned it would attack US forces approaching the strait. The US launched Project Freedom to escort stranded vessels through the waterway.

item.title: ☢️ War on Iran triggers crisis of trust in nuclear non-proliferation treaty
item.description: The US-Israel campaign against Iran has exposed fundamental flaws in the NPT, diplomats say. Iran remained in compliance with the JCPOA before the US withdrawal, raising questions about the value of nuclear restraint.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran says it received US response to latest peace proposal
item.description: Tehran confirmed receiving Washingtons response to its 14-point proposal via Pakistan. Trump said he would likely reject the offer because Iran has not paid a big enough price.

item.title: 🇦🇺🇯🇵 Australia and Japan sign defense and critical minerals agreements
item.description: PMs Albanese and Takaichi signed agreements on defense cooperation, energy security, and critical minerals supply chains. The quasi-alliance aims to counter Chinas growing influence in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸⚔️ Iran Warns US Ships Not to Enter Strait of Hormuz as 'Guide Mission' Launches
item.description: Iran's military warned that any foreign armed force approaching the Strait of Hormuz would be attacked, as the US begins a mission to guide stranded ships through the critical waterway. The standoff continues amid fragile ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇦🇺💰 Australia and Japan Sign Energy Security Pact Amid Middle East Supply Crisis
item.description: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi and Australian PM Anthony Albanese signed agreements deepening cooperation on energy and critical minerals, with Australia providing up to A$1.3 billion for critical mineral projects. The pact aims to secure supply chains disrupted by the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇱🇧⚠️💀 Hezbollah Suffers Heavy Casualties in Lebanon Fighting, Over 2,600 Dead
item.description: Lebanon's health ministry reports more than 2,600 killed since March 2, with around a fifth being women, children and medics, as Hezbollah battles to reverse its fortunes on the Lebanon front.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸⚔️ Iran Warns US Navy to Stay Clear of Hormuz as Trump Launches Ship Escort Mission
item.description: Iran's military warned US forces not to enter the Strait of Hormuz after President Trump announced 'Project Freedom', a US-led operation to guide stranded ships out of the critical waterway. CENTCOM says 15,000 personnel and over 100 aircraft will support the mission.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸📺 Iran War Live: Trump's 'Project Freedom' to Guide Ships Through Hormuz
item.description: CBS News live coverage of the Iran-US standoff as Trump launches operation to guide stranded vessels through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran has threatened to attack any foreign forces entering the waterway.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸⚔️ Iran Threatens US Forces as BBC Live-Updates Hormuz Standoff
item.description: BBC News live blog tracking the Iran-US crisis as Iran threatens to attack US forces approaching the Strait of Hormuz. Trump announced 'Project Freedom' to escort stranded ships, raising tensions in the region.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇺🇸 NATO defense spending rose nearly 20% in 2025 as Germany adds troops
item.description: Germany will add 50,000 troops as NATO defense spending surges. The increase comes amid concerns about US commitment to European security under Trump and ongoing Russian threats.

item.title: 🇪🇺💪 NATO Defense Spending Rose Almost 20% in 2025, DW Reports
item.description: European NATO members increased defense spending by nearly 20% in 2025, with Germany planning to add 50,000 troops. The surge reflects growing concerns about Russian aggression and uncertainty over US security guarantees.

item.title: 🇹🇼🌏 Taiwan Essential for World's Future, Deputy Foreign Minister Says
item.description: Taiwan's Deputy Foreign Minister Francois Wu told Le Monde that the island has become essential for the world's future, highlighting its critical role in semiconductor supply chains and democratic values.

item.title: 🌏💰 Asia's Economic Future Rewired by Global Fragmentation
item.description: Asia Times reports on how global fragmentation is reshaping the region's economic future, with supply chains shifting and countries hedging between US and China in an increasingly polarized world.

item.title: ☢️🇺🇦 IAEA Reports Drone Targeted Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant's Radiation Lab
item.description: The IAEA said a drone targeted the external radiation control laboratory at the Russian-held Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant in southeastern Ukraine, raising safety concerns about Europe's largest nuclear facility.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇹🇼 US Praises Taiwan as Trusted Partner in Ties With Eswatini
item.description: The US State Department praised Taiwan as a trusted and capable partner, highlighting its relationship with Eswatini, one of Taiwan's few remaining diplomatic allies. The statement comes amid ongoing US-China tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚠️ Nobel Laureate Mohammadi Remains in Critical Condition, Foundation Says
item.description: Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi remains in critical condition in Iran, a foundation said, amid concerns about her health following reports of medical neglect in Iranian prisons.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇦🇺 Japan and Australia Deepen Cooperation on Energy and Defense
item.description: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi and Australian PM Anthony Albanese signed agreements on energy security, defense cooperation, and critical minerals, strengthening ties between the two US allies in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: 🇨🇳🤖 AI Helped Shenzhen Judges Handle Cases 50% Faster
item.description: AI tools helped judges in Shenzhen process cases 50% faster, SCMP reports, as China expands the use of artificial intelligence in its legal system to improve efficiency and reduce case backlogs.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 BBC: Iran-US Standoff Over Hormuz Continues
item.description: BBC coverage of the ongoing Iran-US confrontation over the Strait of Hormuz, as both sides trade threats over the critical oil transit chokepoint.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 BBC: Trump's Ship Escort Mission Raises Tensions With Iran
item.description: Trump's announcement of a ship escort mission through the Strait of Hormuz has raised tensions with Iran, which has threatened to attack any foreign forces entering the waterway.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 BBC: Iran's Nuclear Program in Focus Amid US Tensions
item.description: BBC examines Iran's nuclear program as tensions with the US escalate over the Strait of Hormuz and ceasefire negotiations stall.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇪🇺🇺🇦 UK Set to Enter Talks on Joining EU's €105.9 Billion Ukraine Loan
item.description: The UK is set to enter talks on joining the EU's €105.9 billion loan package for Ukraine, marking a significant step in post-Brexit cooperation on supporting Kyiv against Russian aggression.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇫🇮 Ukraine Offers Finland Drone Deal, Zelensky Says
item.description: President Zelensky said Ukraine offered Finland a drone deal during discussions at the European Political Community summit in Yerevan, Armenia, as Ukraine seeks to expand defense partnerships.
